draft.claim: Salesforce's potential moat is switching costs and platform breadth across customer data, workflow, automation, integration, analytics, and ecosystem partners.
draft.mechanism_evidence: Large enterprise customers can embed Salesforce products across customer-facing workflows, making renewal, integration, and multi-cloud expansion the forward-looking durability mechanisms.

moat_mechanism.source_label: Salesforce FY2026 Form 10-K product and platform discussion
moat_mechanism.excerpt_or_summary: The filing describes a broad suite of enterprise cloud applications and platform services used across customer relationship workflows.

historical_economics.draft: FY2025 operating income is positive and margins are high enough to support a cash-generator route, but durability depends on continued renewal, expansion, and disciplined reinvestment.
historical_economics.excerpt_or_summary: The spine carries positive FY2025 revenue and operating income from EDGAR-derived facts.
historical_economics.filing_reference: CRM FY2025 Form 10-K, EDGAR-derived spine inputs

durability_risk.draft: The durability risk is that cloud software competition, customer budget pressure, AI platform shifts, or acquisition complexity weakens renewals and expansion before the SaaS margin profile can compound.
durability_risk.source_label: Salesforce FY2026 Form 10-K competition and technology risks
durability_risk.excerpt_or_summary: Risk factors discuss competitive pressure, enterprise spending cycles, data security, technology change, AI, integration, and operating execution.
